name: PM-KISAN Samman Nidhi
description: Direct income support of Rs 6000 per year to farmer families in 3 installments under the Pradhan Mantri Kisan Samman Nidhi scheme launched in 2019
benefits: Rs 6000 per year in 3 equal installments of Rs 2000 each, directly to bank account via DBT
how_to_apply: Register at pmkisan.gov.in or visit nearest CSC center with Aadhaar and bank details

name: Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana
description: Comprehensive crop insurance scheme providing financial support to farmers suffering crop loss or damage due to natural calamities, pests, and diseases
benefits: Crop insurance at premium of 2% for Kharif crops, 1.5% for Rabi crops, and 5% for commercial/horticulture crops with full sum insured coverage
how_to_apply: Apply through nearest bank branch, CSC center, or the PMFBY portal at pmfby.gov.in before the seasonal deadline

name: Kisan Credit Card
description: Provides farmers with affordable short-term credit for cultivation, post-harvest expenses, and allied agricultural activities through a flexible credit card mechanism
benefits: Crop loans at 4% effective interest rate (7% with 3% interest subvention for timely repayment), credit limit based on land holding and cropping pattern
how_to_apply: Apply at any scheduled commercial bank, cooperative bank, or regional rural bank branch with land records

name: Soil Health Card Scheme
description: Provides soil health cards to farmers with crop-wise nutrient recommendations to improve soil fertility and productivity through balanced use of fertilizers
benefits: Free soil testing and issuance of Soil Health Card every 2 years with recommendations on dosage of fertilizers and soil amendments
how_to_apply: Contact nearest agriculture department office or Krishi Vigyan Kendra, or register at soilhealth.dac.gov.in

name: Pradhan Mantri Krishi Sinchai Yojana
description: Ensures access to protective irrigation for every farm (Har Khet Ko Paani) and improves water use efficiency through micro-irrigation and watershed development
benefits: Up to 55% subsidy on micro-irrigation systems (drip and sprinkler), additional top-up by state governments bringing total subsidy up to 80-90% for small/marginal farmers
how_to_apply: Apply through state agriculture or horticulture department, or register at pmksy.gov.in

name: Paramparagat Krishi Vikas Yojana
description: Promotes organic farming through cluster-based approach, supporting farmers in adoption of organic practices including certification and marketing
benefits: Rs 50000 per hectare over 3 years for organic inputs, seeds, harvesting, and transportation to market. Includes PGS-India organic certification
how_to_apply: Form a cluster of 50 or more farmers through local agriculture department and apply through the Regional Centre of Organic Farming

name: e-NAM (National Agriculture Market)
description: Online trading platform connecting APMCs across the country to create a unified national market for agricultural commodities, enabling transparent price discovery
benefits: Access to transparent online trading across mandis nationwide, real-time price information, direct payment to bank account, reduced intermediary costs
how_to_apply: Register at enam.gov.in using Aadhaar and bank details, or visit nearest e-NAM enabled APMC mandi

name: National Food Security Mission
description: Aims to increase production and productivity of rice, wheat, pulses, coarse cereals, and commercial crops through area expansion and technology demonstrations
benefits: Subsidized certified seeds, integrated nutrient management inputs, resource conservation machinery, and crop demonstrations for rice, wheat, and pulses
how_to_apply: Contact District Agriculture Officer or block-level agriculture office for registration under NFSM components

name: Rashtriya Krishi Vikas Yojana
description: Incentivizes states to enhance public investment in agriculture by providing flexible funding for agriculture and allied sector development projects
benefits: Infrastructure grants for agri-startups, post-harvest management, cold chain development, and value addition projects with up to 50% subsidy
how_to_apply: Apply through state agriculture department or submit proposals through RKVY-RAFTAAR portal for agri-entrepreneurship

name: Sub-Mission on Agricultural Mechanization
description: Promotes farm mechanization by providing subsidies on purchase of agricultural machinery and equipment, with custom hiring centers for small farmers
benefits: 50-80% subsidy on purchase of agricultural machinery and equipment including tractors, harvesters, tillers, and implements for small and marginal farmers
how_to_apply: Apply online at agrimachinery.nic.in or through District Agriculture Officer with required documents

name: PM Kisan Maandhan Yojana
description: Voluntary and contributory pension scheme for small and marginal farmers to provide social security in old age with monthly pension after attaining 60 years
benefits: Guaranteed monthly pension of Rs 3000 after attaining age of 60 years, with equal contribution from government to pension fund
how_to_apply: Register at nearest CSC center or visit pmkmy.gov.in with Aadhaar and bank details. Monthly contribution ranges from Rs 55 to Rs 200 based on age
documents: Bank account details with IFSC code

name: Agriculture Infrastructure Fund
description: Provides medium to long-term financing for investment in post-harvest management and community farming assets through interest subvention and credit guarantee
benefits: 3% per annum interest subvention on loans up to Rs 2 crore for post-harvest infrastructure like warehouses, cold storage, and processing units with CGTMSE credit guarantee
how_to_apply: Apply through any scheduled bank or through the Agriculture Infrastructure Fund portal at agriinfra.dac.gov.in

name: Micro Irrigation Fund
description: Dedicated fund operated through NABARD to support state-level implementation of micro-irrigation projects covering drip and sprinkler irrigation systems
benefits: State-level funding support for expansion of drip and sprinkler irrigation systems, innovative micro-irrigation technologies, and extension activities
how_to_apply: Apply through state agriculture or horticulture department micro-irrigation program under PMKSY-Per Drop More Crop

name: National Beekeeping and Honey Mission
description: Promotes scientific beekeeping for income generation, crop pollination, and production of quality honey and other bee products across the country
benefits: Subsidy on bee colonies, beekeeping equipment, honey processing units, and training programs. Up to Rs 14000 per bee colony set with equipment
how_to_apply: Contact National Bee Board, state agriculture/horticulture department, or Khadi and Village Industries Commission for subsidy application

name: National Mission on Sustainable Agriculture
description: Promotes climate-resilient agricultural practices through soil health management, water use efficiency, and adoption of climate-smart farming technologies
benefits: Support for rainfed area development, soil health management, climate change adaptation in agriculture, and resource conservation technology demonstrations
how_to_apply: Contact District Agriculture Officer or state agriculture department for registration under NMSA components and sub-missions

name: Rythu Bandhu Investment Support Scheme
description: Telangana government's flagship farmer investment support scheme providing direct financial assistance to all land-owning farmers for agricultural investment
benefits: Rs 10000 per acre per year paid in two installments of Rs 5000 each during Vanakalam (Kharif) and Yasangi (Rabi) seasons directly to bank account
how_to_apply: Register at nearest Mee-Seva center or agriculture office with pattadar passbook. Amount credited automatically for registered farmers

name: Rythu Bima (Farmer Life Insurance)
description: Free life insurance scheme for farmers in Telangana providing Rs 5 lakh coverage in case of death due to any cause including natural death
benefits: Rs 5 lakh life insurance coverage for farmers aged 18-59 years, premium fully paid by government, claim amount directly paid to nominee
how_to_apply: Automatically enrolled for Rythu Bandhu beneficiaries. Nominee details updated through agriculture department or Mee-Seva center

name: YSR Rythu Bharosa - PM KISAN
description: Andhra Pradesh government combines state investment support with PM-KISAN to provide Rs 13500 per year to all farmer families for agricultural investment
benefits: Rs 13500 per year total — Rs 7500 state component (paid in Kharif season) plus Rs 6000 from PM-KISAN, directly credited to bank account
how_to_apply: Register at nearest RBK (Rythu Bharosa Kendra) or village secretariat with land documents and Aadhaar

name: YSR Free Crop Insurance
description: Andhra Pradesh government pays the full farmer share of crop insurance premium under PMFBY, making crop insurance completely free for farmers in the state
benefits: Free crop insurance with zero premium for farmers — state government pays farmer share of premium under PMFBY for all notified crops
how_to_apply: Apply through nearest RBK (Rythu Bharosa Kendra) or bank branch during enrollment window before crop season

name: Tamil Nadu Free Borewell Scheme
description: Tamil Nadu state government provides free borewell drilling to small and marginal farmers for irrigation purpose to improve agricultural productivity
benefits: Free borewell drilling up to 200 feet depth with casing pipes and motor pump set, fully funded by state government for eligible farmers
how_to_apply: Apply at District Agriculture Office or through the Tamil Nadu Agriculture Department's online portal with land documents

name: Tamil Nadu Crop Insurance Scheme
description: State crop insurance scheme operated alongside PMFBY to provide additional crop loss compensation to farmers in Tamil Nadu for notified crops and areas
benefits: Subsidized crop insurance premium with state top-up compensation beyond PMFBY coverage for crop losses due to drought, flood, and pest attacks
how_to_apply: Enroll at nearest cooperative bank, commercial bank branch, or through common service center during the enrollment period
documents: Sowing certificate from Village Administrative Officer

name: Raitha Siri (Organic Farming Scheme)
description: Karnataka government scheme promoting organic farming among small and marginal farmers through financial assistance for bio-inputs and organic certification
benefits: Financial assistance of Rs 6500 per acre for adoption of organic farming practices, free bio-inputs, vermicompost units, and training on organic cultivation
how_to_apply: Apply through Raitha Samparka Kendra (farmer contact center) or district agriculture department office in Karnataka
documents: RTC (Record of Rights, Tenancy and Crops)

name: Krishi Bhagya Scheme
description: Karnataka scheme to help farmers in dry and rain-dependent areas by creating farm ponds, polyethylene lining, and providing diesel pump sets for protective irrigation
benefits: 80% subsidy on construction of farm ponds with polyethylene lining, diesel pump sets, sprinkler/drip irrigation sets for rainfed area farmers
how_to_apply: Apply through Raitha Samparka Kendra or district agriculture department. Scheme implemented through Department of Agriculture, Karnataka

name: Namo Shetkari Mahasanman Nidhi
description: Maharashtra government's farmer welfare scheme providing Rs 6000 per year as state top-up in addition to PM-KISAN benefits for farmer families in the state
benefits: Rs 6000 per year state top-up paid in 3 installments of Rs 2000 each, in addition to Rs 6000 from PM-KISAN, total Rs 12000 per year
how_to_apply: Register through existing PM-KISAN portal. Eligible PM-KISAN beneficiaries in Maharashtra automatically enrolled

name: Maharashtra State Crop Insurance Scheme
description: State crop insurance scheme complementing PMFBY with additional coverage and reduced premium burden for Maharashtra farmers for notified crops
benefits: Subsidized crop insurance with Re 1 premium for farmers. State bears the remaining premium cost. Coverage for drought, flood, pest, and disease damage
how_to_apply: Enroll through nearest bank branch, CSC center, or through the Maharashtra agriculture department portal during season enrollment window

name: Mukhya Mantri Kisan Kalyan Yojana
description: Madhya Pradesh scheme providing Rs 4000 per year as state supplement to PM-KISAN beneficiaries for direct income support to farmer families
benefits: Rs 4000 per year in 2 installments of Rs 2000 each, in addition to PM-KISAN benefits, directly credited to bank account via DBT
how_to_apply: Eligible PM-KISAN beneficiaries in Madhya Pradesh are automatically enrolled. New farmers register through district agriculture office

name: Bhavantar Bhugtan Yojana (MP)
description: Madhya Pradesh price deficiency payment scheme that compensates farmers when market prices fall below the Minimum Support Price for notified crops
benefits: Difference between MSP (or modal price) and actual selling price paid directly to farmer's bank account for registered produce sold in mandis
how_to_apply: Register on e-Uparjan portal (mpeuparjan.nic.in) before the selling season. Sell produce at registered mandi and claim price difference

name: UP Kisan Samman Nidhi (State Top-up)
description: Uttar Pradesh supplementary farmer income support providing additional direct benefit transfer to complement PM-KISAN benefits for state farmers
benefits: Additional state income support to PM-KISAN beneficiaries, assistance with crop procurement at MSP, and subsidized agricultural inputs
how_to_apply: Register through district agriculture office or PM-KISAN portal. Existing PM-KISAN beneficiaries receive state benefits automatically

name: UP Free Borewell/Nalkoop Yojana
description: Uttar Pradesh government scheme to provide free borewells to small and marginal farmers in identified blocks for irrigation development
benefits: Free borewell drilling and installation with pump set for irrigation, fully funded by state government for small and marginal farmers
how_to_apply: Apply through Block Development Office or district agriculture/minor irrigation department with land documents

name: Punjab Crop Diversification Programme
description: Punjab government incentivizes farmers to shift from paddy monoculture to less water-intensive crops like maize, cotton, pulses, and oilseeds
benefits: Rs 12000 per acre incentive for diversifying from paddy to alternative crops, free seeds, technical guidance, and assured procurement support
how_to_apply: Register at block agriculture office or through Punjab Mandi Board portal before Kharif season sowing

name: Punjab Tubewell Subsidy Scheme
description: Punjab government provides subsidized tubewells and submersible pump sets to small and marginal farmers for assured irrigation facilities
benefits: Up to 80% subsidy on installation of tubewell and submersible pump set for small and marginal farmers in identified blocks
how_to_apply: Apply through district agriculture department or block agriculture officer's office with required documents

name: Bhavantar Bharpayee Yojana (Haryana)
description: Haryana government price deficiency payment scheme compensating farmers for losses when market prices of horticultural crops fall below protected prices
benefits: Difference between protected price and actual selling price paid directly to farmer's bank account for registered vegetables and fruits sold in mandis
how_to_apply: Register on Meri Fasal Mera Byora portal (fasal.haryana.gov.in) with crop and land details before selling season

name: Haryana Solar Water Pump Scheme
description: Haryana government provides solar-powered water pumps to farmers at subsidized rates for irrigation, reducing dependence on diesel and electricity
benefits: Up to 75% subsidy on solar water pump sets (2HP to 10HP capacity). Reduces irrigation costs and provides reliable power for water pumping
how_to_apply: Apply through Saral Haryana portal (saralharyana.gov.in) or district agriculture office. Pumps supplied through empaneled vendors

name: Mukhya Mantri Krishak Sathi Yojana
description: Rajasthan accident insurance scheme providing financial assistance to farmers and farm laborers in case of death or disability due to farm accidents
benefits: Rs 2 lakh in case of death, Rs 50000 for permanent partial disability, and Rs 5000-25000 for finger/limb injuries due to farming accidents
how_to_apply: File claim through district agriculture officer or e-Mitra center within 6 months of the accident with medical and FIR reports

name: Rajasthan Micro Irrigation Subsidy Scheme
description: Rajasthan state scheme supplementing central PMKSY to provide higher subsidies on drip and sprinkler irrigation to conserve water in arid regions
benefits: Up to 70% subsidy on drip irrigation and 60% on sprinkler systems for general category, up to 75% for SC/ST farmers, through combined central-state funding
how_to_apply: Apply through Raj Kisan Sathi portal (rajkisan.rajasthan.gov.in) or district agriculture/horticulture department office

name: Kisan Suryodaya Yojana
description: Gujarat government initiative to provide daytime electricity to farmers for irrigation through dedicated solar-powered feeder lines and grid infrastructure
benefits: Daytime electricity supply (5 AM to 9 PM) for agricultural purposes at subsidized rates through dedicated solar feeder infrastructure
how_to_apply: Apply through nearest PGVCL/MGVCL/DGVCL/UGVCL office or through iKhedut portal (ikhedut.gujarat.gov.in) for new agricultural connection

name: Gujarat State Crop Insurance Scheme
description: Gujarat state scheme working alongside PMFBY providing crop insurance coverage with state-subsidized premium for all major crops in the state
benefits: Crop insurance coverage at subsidized premium rates, state top-up to PMFBY compensation for major crop losses due to natural calamities
how_to_apply: Enroll through bank branch, CSC center, or through iKhedut portal during the seasonal enrollment window

name: Krishak Bandhu Scheme
description: West Bengal government's direct income support scheme providing financial assistance to all enrolled farmers in the state for agricultural investment
benefits: Rs 10000 per year for farmers with more than 1 acre and Rs 4000 per year for farmers with up to 1 acre, paid in two installments
how_to_apply: Register at Krishak Bandhu portal (krishakbandhu.net) or through local Block Development Office with land documents

name: Krishak Bandhu Death Benefit Scheme
description: West Bengal scheme providing ex-gratia lump sum payment to the family of registered farmers in case of death from any cause between ages 18-60
benefits: Rs 2 lakh one-time payment to nominee/family of deceased enrolled farmer, no premium required from farmer, fully funded by state government
how_to_apply: Nominee files claim through Block Development Office or Krishak Bandhu portal with death certificate within 90 days of death

name: Bihar Rajya Fasal Sahayata Yojana
description: Bihar state crop damage compensation scheme providing direct financial assistance to farmers for crop loss without requiring premium payment
benefits: Rs 7500 per hectare for up to 20% crop damage, Rs 10000 per hectare for more than 20% crop damage, maximum 2 hectares per farmer
how_to_apply: Register on Bihar DBT Agriculture portal (dbtagriculture.bihar.gov.in) during the crop season and file claim if crop damage occurs

name: Bihar Diesel Subsidy Scheme
description: Bihar government provides diesel subsidy to small and marginal farmers for irrigation during Kharif and Rabi seasons to reduce cultivation costs
benefits: Rs 75 per litre diesel subsidy for up to 12 litres per acre for irrigating crops, paid directly to bank account on submission of diesel purchase receipt
how_to_apply: Register on Bihar DBT Agriculture portal (dbtagriculture.bihar.gov.in) and upload diesel purchase receipts with crop details

name: KALIA (Krushak Assistance for Livelihood and Income Augmentation)
description: Odisha government scheme providing financial assistance to small and marginal farmers for cultivation, livelihood support, and vulnerable agricultural households
benefits: Rs 12500 per year (Rs 5000 Kharif + Rs 5000 Rabi + Rs 2500 additional support) per farmer family for crop cultivation and allied activities
how_to_apply: Register at kalia.odisha.gov.in or through district agriculture office with land and identity documents

name: KALIA Life Insurance Scheme
description: Odisha government provides life insurance coverage to KALIA beneficiary farmers at no cost, with premium fully borne by the state government
benefits: Rs 2 lakh life insurance coverage for KALIA beneficiaries aged 18-50 years, Rs 2 lakh additional personal accident cover, premium paid by state government
how_to_apply: Automatically enrolled for KALIA beneficiaries through state agriculture department. Verify enrollment at kalia.odisha.gov.in

name: Kerala Vegetable Development Programme
description: Kerala state programme promoting commercial vegetable cultivation through financial assistance for seeds, inputs, and infrastructure to achieve self-sufficiency
benefits: Subsidized seeds and planting materials, financial assistance up to Rs 30000 per hectare for vegetable cultivation, polyhouse and net house construction support
how_to_apply: Apply through Krishi Bhavan (block agriculture office) or through the Kerala Agriculture Department's online portal

name: Kerala Coconut Palm Insurance Scheme
description: Kerala state coconut palm insurance scheme providing coverage against damage or loss of coconut palms due to natural calamities and diseases
benefits: Insurance coverage up to Rs 32000 per coconut palm for bearing trees and Rs 16000 for non-bearing trees damaged by natural calamities or diseases
how_to_apply: Apply through Coconut Development Board (CDB) office, Krishi Bhavan, or through CDB online portal with details of coconut palms
